title: How do the plugsuits work
wikitext:
[]: How do the plugsuits work?             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri May 02, 2008 11:11 pm Or, more specifically, how the heck do the pilots get in and out of them? There are these pieces around the wrists that ought to make getting one's hands through the suit's arms impossible. And the neck of the suit I find the most puzzling, and it's sort of the key to everything. It looks totally seamless front and back, but it would need to open up somewhere to let the head through, wouldn't it? I watched the two cuts in Webuild 1.0 of Rei putting the plugsuit on more times than can be considered healthy, but these didn't really elucidate matters. Initially the suit looks like it has no shoulders or neck to it at all, and then Rei pulls the neck of the suit out of nowhere, and it somehow becomes seamless in front. I'm confused... When Asuka is getting into her suit in episode 08, the thing does have a neck and shoulders, but I still wouldn't be able to explain how the suit "comes together". Would post screenies if I weren't lazy, but I bet Zuggy already has them ready. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sat May 03, 2008 12:22 am First thing that comes to mind is an organized group of nanomachines.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sat May 03, 2008 12:45 am well, from the episode 8 scene, it seems like it starts out open, and is baggy until she pushes the magic button that not only tightens the suit material, but also vacuums the air out. of course, it doesnt seem quite obvious how the shapeless edge becomes the well defined. Especially interesting is if you take a look at the suit Asuka gives Shinji, it does carry the neck part. I guess its kind of a continuity error? Of course, since the material can shape-shift with the push of a button, it could just be that there is some kind of stretching that you can do?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sat May 03, 2008 1:06 am --

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat May 03, 2008 3:31 am Unstable molecules! That's been working for the Fantastic Four for the last 45 years...

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Sat May 03, 2008 4:29 am Hmm...one question - what are the plug suits made of anyway? At first I was thinking some kind of special plastic or composite material. Because, the material needed to produce plug suits needs to be flexible enough so that it can tighten itself up to accommodate the pilot.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Sat May 03, 2008 7:41 am The plug suit is a form-fitting full body garment which the Eva pilots wear to aid in synchronization with their Evas. The suits are normally bulky but shrink to conform to the pilot's body through a mechanism housed in the wrists of the suits. The suits contain devices which help monitor the pilot's status and give medical aid, such as defibrillation or CPR. No two suits are alike: each suit bears the numerical designation of its corresponding Eva on the chest and back and is roughly color-coordinated with its corresponding Eva. Rei's plug suit can be removed; whether or not this is a deliberate design or a medical necessity (due to injuries to her arms) is not revealed. From the feet to the neck, the Plug Suit is one-piece and has a structure similar to that of a wetsuit. It fits rather loosely on the body until a switch is activated on the wrist, causing the suit to shrink perfectly against the body of the pilot. The suit also comes with two head pieces, which Asuka chooses to wear as decoration. The Plug Suit, although helpful, is not required for the operation of an Evangelion Unit. Shinji in particular, whose Sync Ratio is naturally high, has piloted Evangelion Unit-01 on more than one occasion without this suit. Thats all the information I could dig up tonight. Ill have a look in Eva chronicals and see if ther is any info there

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Sat May 03, 2008 9:42 am Maybe the mechanism used to shrink the suits is a pressurization system, perhaps?

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sat May 03, 2008 10:11 am The suits are made of plain old cotton, and when the wrist switch is pressed water is poured all over the pilots body causing the suit to stick to the skin in an inappropriate way. See wet t-shirt effect. Either that or a mixture of magic and fan-service.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sat May 03, 2008 10:25 am a pressurization system alone wouldnt cut it. If it was just a vacuuming effect, then there would be lots of wrinkles. Thus, it has to be that the material itself contracts (electric current causes molecules to rearrange, amount of charge determines by how much?). The picture you have, Zuggy, seems to make sense, although wouldnt the suit have to be really baggy to be able to fit your entire body in and then loop the neck piece around your head? Freeze frames from Ep. 20 arent much help either, since it shows the closed plug suit

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat May 03, 2008 10:28 am I would think that that compression/decompression mechanism (See episode 08 with Asuka, 05 with Rei) would have something to do with it. Basically the plugsuits are loose and easy to get into (akin to a teeshirt; and maybe the plugsuit material is stretchy) but then it stays put once you press that wrist compression doo-hicky. It's a really good question but the above is the best I can come up with. (Though I wonder if the Plugsuits are as erm "comfortable" as Plugsuit Fetish would lead you to believe. )

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat May 03, 2008 10:43 am I watched the two cuts in Webuild 1.0 of Rei putting the plugsuit on more times than can be considered healthy,

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Sat May 03, 2008 10:46 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Though I wonder if the Plugsuits are as erm "comfortable" as Plugsuit Fetish would lead you to believe.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat May 03, 2008 1:02 pm sadsadshinji wrote: a pressurization system alone wouldnt cut it. If it was just a vacuuming effect, then there would be lots of wrinkles. Thus, it has to be that the material itself contracts (electric current causes molecules to rearrange, amount of charge determines by how much?).

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat May 03, 2008 1:06 pm V wrote: I just gathered that you step in like a wetsuit

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sat May 03, 2008 2:02 pm I think its just that the suits are relatively loose (including the wrists), you step in, the material contracts with the button push. Looking at Ep. 8 again, the plug suit is rather ill defined when it is around her shoulders (which would show how the suit opens). If you look carefully at 10:16, you can see that there are some white lines on the inside of the suit. At 10:18, when she turns around, you can see that the little backpack thing is pretty much at the edge of the top, supporting Zuggy's picture. Still, at 10:27, we cant see a gap or opening anymore, although it seems like her hair is coming out of the back-opening, not the neck. If anything, the wrists seem to be wider on the inside, allowing for the hand to get into the glove area. Concerning how the material works, I guess the explanation would have to be:"its an anime"

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat May 03, 2008 3:02 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I wonder if the Plugsuits are as erm "comfortable" as Plugsuit Fetish would lead you to believe.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Sat May 03, 2008 4:17 pm Nanomachines.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sat May 03, 2008 5:40 pm Just watched "You are not alone", and I think that the neck breaks open in the back, and probably has some kind of ring inside (so that it snaps back into shape once its around the neck), because Rei is seen adjusting something along the neck area

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat May 03, 2008 6:42 pm I noticed what sadsadShinji said, too. There probably is something adjustable around the neck, then. (I guess the series didn't make this clear.) @ NAW-kun:

When Asuka is getting into her suit in episode 08, the thing does have a neck and shoulders, but I still wouldn't be able to explain how the suit "comes together". Would post screenies if I weren't lazy, but I bet Zuggy already has them ready. ;)
